title: Coordination Claim
description: Durable per-resource lease record. The SSOT for who owns a shared workspace resource (git worktree, evidence run directory, workflow run record) at any given time. See docs/COORDINATION.md for lifecycle + 'Expiry Authority' semantics. Runtime implementation in FAZ-B PR-B1 (ao_kernel/coordination/).

claim_id.description: UUIDv4. Unique per claim instance; two successive claims on the same resource have different claim_ids (whereas fencing_token increases monotonically per resource — see fencing-state.schema.v1.json).

owner_agent_id.description: Agent that holds the claim. Matches the identifier used by the caller (typically the adapter_id of the invoking adapter or a workflow-level agent identifier).

resource_id.description: Resource being claimed. Callers MUST NOT encode path separators or wildcards; the policy's claim_resource_patterns allowlist decides what shapes are permitted. v1: single resource per claim (multi-resource atomic acquire is deferred to FAZ-C per CNS-028v2 iter-2 absorb).

fencing_token.description: Per-resource monotonic token. Read from (and advanced in) fencing-state.v1.json on every acquire and takeover. Never reset, never wraps (Python int is unbounded). Stale-token detection at run_step entry raises ClaimStaleFencingError.

acquired_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this claim instance was first acquired (does NOT reset on heartbeat).

heartbeat_at.description: Source-of-truth liveness timestamp. effective_expires_at = heartbeat_at + policy.expiry_seconds (computed at evaluation time). See docs/COORDINATION.md §6 Expiry Authority.

expires_at.description: DERIVED field. Mutators populate this for validation/debug visibility but it is NOT the source of truth — source = heartbeat_at + policy.expiry_seconds. Readers must recompute rather than trust this field past a heartbeat.

revision.description: CAS revision hash, computed over the canonical JSON projection of the claim record with this field omitted (mirrors canonical_store.store_revision pattern). Mutators pass expected_revision to guard against concurrent writes.
